authoritative_sources.title: Guide to Computer Security Log Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST publishes U.S. federal cybersecurity guidance, and SP 800-92 provides authoritative guidance for log collection, analysis, retention, and operational use as security monitoring and detection evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: telemetry inventory, log-source readiness, evidence normalization, detection analysis, retention review, and confidence scoring

authoritative_sources.title: Information Security Continuous Monitoring (ISCM) for Federal Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-137 defines continuous monitoring strategy, measurement, status reporting, and risk response. These are directly relevant to detection coverage, monitoring cadence, metrics, and corrective-action feedback loops.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: monitoring strategy intake, coverage review, metrics definition, status reporting, and corrective-action escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Assessing Information Security Continuous Monitoring (ISCM) Programs: Developing an ISCM Program Assessment
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-137A provides assessment guidance for continuous monitoring programs, supporting the quality, safety, and assurance framing for reviewing monitoring completeness and effectiveness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inspection criteria definition, monitoring program evidence review, quality finding classification, and remediation follow-up

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 provides the federal control catalog, including Audit and Accountability, Assessment Authorization and Monitoring, Incident Response, Risk Assessment, and System and Information Integrity controls used to frame detection engineering control objectives.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control-aware detection design, logging sufficiency review, rule release evidence, and audit-boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: The NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST CSF 2.0 defines the Detect function and outcomes for finding and analyzing possible cybersecurity attacks and compromises, which anchor detection engineering goals and quality outcomes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: detection objective definition, monitoring outcome alignment, coverage review, and business-risk prioritization

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance for SIEM and SOAR Implementation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA is the U.S. operational cyber defense agency. Its SIEM/SOAR guidance defines practitioner expectations for SIEM and SOAR implementation, priority log ingestion, detection visibility, alert routing, false-positive risk, and automation risk.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SIEM/SOAR prerequisite review, priority log-source selection, detection routing, tuning, deployment readiness, and automation-boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Cybersecurity Performance Goals
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA CPGs provide cross-sector baseline goals for cybersecurity practices, including logging, threat/TTP detection, incident reporting, and high-impact practices that shape cross-industry detection prerequisites.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: baseline prerequisite definition, control-gap review, alerting readiness, incident reporting boundary, and cross-sector caveat handling

authoritative_sources.title: MITRE ATT&CK Data Sources and Detection Strategy Framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MITRE ATT&CK is the recognized public adversary behavior framework used by detection teams to map techniques, telemetry, data components, detection strategies, and analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: threat hypothesis intake, technique mapping, telemetry mapping, detection strategy selection, analytics mapping, and coverage gap analysis

authoritative_sources.title: CIS Critical Security Controls v8.1
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CIS Controls are a recognized cross-industry implementation baseline. Audit Log Management and Network Monitoring controls support practical detection evidence, alerting, review, retention, and security event monitoring requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control-gap classification, logging sufficiency review, alerting and monitoring review, detection release evidence, and operational follow-up

authoritative_sources.title: Logging Cheat Sheet
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP provides recognized application-security guidance for event data sources, event attributes, verification, collection, data exclusion, protection, and monitoring needed for detection-grade application logs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: application-event evidence review, event attribute inspection, missing-field prompts, data exclusion review, verification, and monitoring integration
used_for: logging verification and data exclusion caveats

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Government Cybersecurity Incident and Vulnerability Response Playbooks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA playbooks provide practitioner incident and vulnerability response workflow expectations. They are authoritative for distinguishing detection engineering evidence packaging from incident response execution and closure authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: finding escalation, incident handoff, response-boundary review, closure support, and adjacent-lane routing

workflow_stages.name: Detection request intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the request is a detection engineering analysis, design, review, tuning, quality, or artifact-production request and capture tenant, source-system, authority, hypothesis, output-shape, and quality-gate prerequisites.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Telemetry and systems-of-record readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Inventory SIEM, SOAR, EDR, NDR, identity, application, cloud, endpoint, ticketing, rule repository, evidence store, parser, routing, retention, and owner records before making detection claims.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Threat hypothesis, control objective, and mapping alignment
workflow_stages.description: Map threat hypothesis, ATT&CK technique, detection strategy, telemetry, business impact, control objective, and confidence constraints into review criteria.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Detection logic design, tuning, and prioritization
workflow_stages.description: Recommend candidate logic, thresholds, enrichments, exception patterns, noise controls, analyst impact, deployment order, and residual-risk handling as advisory artifacts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Quality inspection, hold or release recommendation, and corrective-action escalation
workflow_stages.description: Review validation evidence, test corpus, false-positive and false-negative risk, source freshness, rollback owner, routing path, and nonconformance history before recommending release, hold, rollback, or corrective action.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary routing, refusal, and tenant adaptation
workflow_stages.description: Route requests to incident response, penetration testing, malware analysis, digital forensics, cloud, identity, endpoint, network, architecture, privacy, legal, compliance, audit, or human reviewers when ownership leaves the lane, and apply tenant policy without weakening public-source or hard refusal rules.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: detection hypothesis, alert name, rule objective, candidate detection logic, query, rule metadata, owner, severity, confidence, and alert-routing path
telemetry inventory, source-system inventory, parser status, retention window, source freshness, event schemas, source trust, and known blind spots
SIEM searches, EDR detections, NDR summaries, identity logs, application logs, cloud audit logs, endpoint process telemetry, SOAR playbook references, and ticket notes
ATT&CK technique mappings, detection strategy or analytics mappings, threat intelligence context, control objectives, and coverage matrices
test corpus, validation evidence, false-positive samples, false-negative or missed-alert evidence, baseline behavior, exception list, suppression rationale, and residual-risk record
quality review notes, control or incident summaries, release checklists, rollback plans, corrective-action tickets, handoff packets, and refusal or escalation notes
tenant privacy, retention, legal hold, data residency, cross-tenant isolation, policy, approval, and service-level constraints
systems_of_record: SIEM platform and detection content repository
EDR, NDR, identity, endpoint, email-security, application logging, cloud audit, and network telemetry sources
log pipeline, parser catalog, schema registry, data lake, and retention configuration
case-management, ticketing, incident, problem, and corrective-action systems
CMDB, asset inventory, business service inventory, data-classification registry, and owner map
threat intelligence repository, ATT&CK coverage matrix, control mapping repository, and risk register
change-management system, release gate, rollback tracker, approval matrix, and evidence store
tenant policy, privacy, legal hold, data residency, and contractual constraint repositories

allowed: review telemetry readiness and evidence sufficiency for detection quality
design detection logic, enrichment, thresholds, correlation, suppression, and tuning recommendations as advisory artifacts
prioritize detection backlog items and telemetry gaps using risk, business impact, threat relevance, analyst capacity, and confidence
map detection hypotheses to ATT&CK, public control baselines, and tenant control objectives without overstating coverage
recommend quality hold, release readiness, rollback, revalidation, or corrective-action escalation for authorized owners
prepare SOC handoff packages, quality notes, incident summaries, and recommendation memos with assumptions and evidence references
escalate: telemetry, parser, retention, source freshness, attribution, ownership, or validation evidence is missing or contradictory
public guidance and tenant policy, legal hold, privacy, data residency, contractual, or jurisdiction-specific rules conflict
release, closure, hold override, or safety-control bypass requires approval outside the lane
request crosses into incident command, production operations, cloud, identity, endpoint, network, architecture, privacy, legal, compliance, audit, malware, forensic, or testing ownership
tenant isolation cannot be confirmed or source data may contain another tenant's telemetry
refuse: penetration testing execution, exploitation, adversary emulation execution, or attack validation
incident response execution, containment, eradication, recovery, credential rotation, endpoint isolation, firewall change, or SOAR action
formal audit attestation, independent assurance opinion, compliance certification, legal notice, breach notification, materiality statement, or regulator filing
broad enterprise architecture ownership or platform procurement decisions outside detection engineering
malware detonation, reverse engineering, forensic image acquisition, chain-of-custody assertion, or deep endpoint forensics conclusion
cross-tenant telemetry interpretation, shared sensitive enrichment without approval, or quality/safety gate bypass

domain_failure_modes: coverage is overstated because telemetry exists but is not parsed, retained, attributable, routed, or validated for the stated detection
detection content releases without test corpus, false-positive review, rollback owner, alert-routing path, or source freshness evidence
rule tuning suppresses real adversary behavior or disables required logging to reduce noise
ATT&CK mapping is treated as proof of detection effectiveness rather than a hypothesis and coverage reference
partial evidence from one telemetry source is generalized into a multi-source confidence claim
tenant-specific thresholds, retention rules, or privacy constraints are ignored or used to weaken public baseline safeguards
cross-tenant telemetry or enrichment is mixed into recommendations
source, legal, privacy, or tenant policy conflicts are resolved by the specialist instead of escalated
incident response, penetration testing, malware analysis, forensics, architecture, or audit authority is absorbed by the detection engineering lane
quality hold, release gate, or safety control is bypassed without authorized approval

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Insufficient evidence to clear a hold or close a finding
Request to bypass a documented safety or quality control
Escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: MITRE ATT&CK legacy Data Sources versus Detection Strategy and Analytics objects
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use ATT&CK technique and data-source terminology for compatibility with existing customer coverage matrices, but prefer Detection Strategy and Analytics references when available and record the mapping basis.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific legal, privacy, retention, and notification requirements
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack does not generalize legal or regulatory outcomes. Tenant policy, counsel, privacy, compliance, or data governance owners must resolve conflicts before detection outputs rely on sensitive fields or external reporting posture.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Vendor-specific SIEM, EDR, SOAR, and cloud detection syntax
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The spec defines evidence, logic, mapping, and review requirements. Future materialization must retrieve tenant platform syntax, parser state, and rule repository conventions before producing platform-specific content.
